articles.title: HKU Launches Hong Kong's First Osteoporosis Screening Programme
articles.summary: The University of Hong Kong (HKU) has introduced a brand-new health programme that screens people for osteoporosis — a condition where bones become weak and break easily. This is the very first city-wide screening effort of its kind in Hong Kong. Osteoporosis is especially common among older adults, and many people do not know they have it until a bone breaks. Early detection means doctors can help patients strengthen their bones before serious injuries happen. Health experts hope the programme will encourage more Hong Kong residents, especially elderly people, to take better care of their bone health and visit doctors for check-ups.

lv1: What does the new HKU programme screen people for? （港大的新計劃為市民篩查什麼疾病？）
Which university launched this new health screening programme in Hong Kong? （哪間大學推出了這個新的健康篩查計劃？）
lv2: Why is it important to detect osteoporosis early, before any bones are broken? （為什麼在骨骼折斷之前及早發現骨質疏鬆症很重要？）
Who do you think would benefit the most from this screening programme, and why? （你認為誰最能受益於這個篩查計劃？原因是什麼？）
lv3: Should the government make bone health screening free and available to all Hong Kong residents? Give reasons for and against. （政府是否應該為所有香港市民提供免費骨骼健康篩查？請提出支持和反對的理由。）
As Hong Kong's population gets older, what other health programmes do you think the city needs most, and how should they be funded? （隨著香港人口老齡化，你認為城市最需要哪些健康計劃，又應如何籌集資金？）

articles.title: US Accuses Chinese AI Firm Moonshot AI of Breaking Rules Over Kimi K3 Model
articles.summary: The United States government has accused a Chinese artificial intelligence company called Moonshot AI of developing its latest AI model, Kimi K3, in a way that breaks American rules. China has strongly pushed back, calling the accusations completely groundless. Moonshot AI is one of China's rising technology companies, and Kimi K3 is its newest and most powerful AI assistant. This dispute is the latest example of growing tension between the US and China in the field of technology. Both countries are competing to lead the world in AI development, and disagreements about rules and fairness are becoming more common.

lv1: What is the name of the Chinese AI company that the US has accused of breaking rules? （美國指控哪家中國人工智能公司違規？）
What is the name of the AI model at the centre of this dispute? （這場爭議中的人工智能模型叫什麼名字？）
lv2: Why do you think the United States and China are competing so hard to be leaders in artificial intelligence? （你認為為什麼美中兩國都在努力爭奪人工智能領域的領先地位？）
How might disagreements between the US and China over technology affect people in Hong Kong or other parts of Asia? （美中兩國在科技方面的分歧，可能如何影響香港或亞洲其他地區的人？）
lv3: Should one country be allowed to make rules that affect how technology companies in other countries operate? Discuss different perspectives. （一個國家是否應該有權制定影響其他國家科技公司運作的規則？請從不同角度討論。）

Is it fair for countries to restrict the development of AI technology in other nations? What are the possible benefits and risks of an 'AI race' between superpowers? （限制其他國家發展人工智能技術是否公平？大國之間的「人工智能競賽」有哪些潛在的好處和風險？）

articles.title: 國泰航空預計今年上半年盈利創歷史次高　載客量大增
articles.summary: 國泰航空宣布，預計今年上半年的盈利將高達65億港元，成為公司歷史上第二高的半年業績。公司指出，旅客人數較去年同期增加了約17%，反映暑假及旅遊旺季的需求持續強勁。管理層對未來前景表示樂觀，認為暑假期間航班需求依然旺盛。國泰的佳績顯示香港航空業在近年挑戰後正逐步復甦，對帶動本地旅遊及經濟發展有正面影響。

lv1: 國泰航空預計今年上半年最高可賺多少億港元？
與去年同期相比，國泰的旅客人數增加了多少百分比？
lv2: 為什麼旅客人數增加會令航空公司的盈利上升？
香港航空業的復甦，對香港整體經濟有什麼好處？
lv3: 航空業的增長對環境有什麼影響？我們應該如何在經濟發展和環境保護之間取得平衡？
如果你是國泰航空的管理層，你會採取什麼措施吸引更多旅客，同時減少對環境的影響？

articles.title: 動漫電玩節明日灣仔會展揭幕　一連五日吸引年輕人參與
articles.summary: 一年一度的香港動漫電玩節將於明日（7月24日）起在灣仔香港會議展覽中心正式開幕，活動為期五天。動漫電玩節是本港其中一個最受年輕人歡迎的大型文化活動，每年吸引大批動漫迷及遊戲愛好者參加。活動設有動漫、電子遊戲、模型及周邊產品等展區，為市民帶來豐富的娛樂體驗，同時有助推廣香港本地的創意文化產業發展。

lv1: 動漫電玩節在哪個場館舉行？
今屆動漫電玩節一共舉行多少天？
lv2: 為什麼動漫電玩節這類文化活動對年輕人來說很重要？
你認為舉辦大型文化活動如何幫助推動香港的經濟發展？
lv3: 有人認為電子遊戲和動漫只是娛樂，對學習沒有幫助；也有人認為它們能培養創意和解難能力。你同意哪個觀點？請提出理由。
香港應該如何進一步發展本地的創意及文化產業，使其在國際上更具競爭力？
